personality_one_liner: A dynamic, tonal-layered financial interface that uses expressive teal accents, rounded containers, and fluid transitions to make money management feel approachable and intelligent.

spacing.rule: Generous padding around tonal containers. Use spacing md (12) or lg (16) between stacked components. Grouping should feel natural and soft, no tight clustering.

do's: Use subtle glassmorphism on the bottom tab bar and sticky headers over scrolling content.
Apply translucent scrims (≥70% opacity base) over busy charts or background elements to ensure readability.
dont's: Do not overuse glass on standard list items or small chips. Rely on tonal elevation for standard cards instead.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: Central hub showing a massive primary balance and quick cash flow summaries.

states.empty: Empty wallet illustration with 'No transactions yet. Start building your nest.'
states.error: Readable error text with a 'Retry' action over surfaceSecondary.

screens.layout: Hero section at the top uses a tonal layered card (surfaceSecondary -> surfaceTertiary) with display typography for the balance. Below are Income/Expense tonal chips. Followed by a 'Recent Activity' list with progressive disclosure. Floating Action Button (FAB) at the bottom right for Add Transaction.

screens.name: Transactions
screens.purpose: A detailed chronological list of all financial activities.

states.loading: Skeleton list rows mimicking the progressive disclosure layout.
states.empty: Illustration with 'Your transaction history is clear.'
states.error: Error message with a Retry button.

screens.layout: Sticky filter pill-row at the top. List rows focus on Merchant Name (lg), Amount (bold), and a Category Icon. Single tap on a row opens a @gorhom/bottom-sheet for details (date, time, location, edit/delete actions) instead of a separate screen.

screens.name: Add Transaction
screens.purpose: Quick, single-decision entry for logging income or expenses.

states.error: Inline validation text under inputs using the error token.

screens.layout: Ideally rendered as a full-screen modal or tall bottom sheet. Features large numeric input for Amount. Uses segmented controls for Type (Income/Expense/Transfer) and tonal chips for Categories. 'Save' CTA is pinned to the bottom using KeyboardAvoidingView.

screens.name: Budget
screens.purpose: Tells a data story about spending pace and limits using dynamic bars.

states.empty: Illustration: 'Set up your first budget to track spending.'
states.error: Failed to load budget data. Retry button.

screens.layout: Stacked cards for each category. Instead of just raw numbers, use predictive text ('On track to save ₹1,500'). Budget bars use dynamic widths with primary or warning/error colors based on threshold.

screens.name: Reports
screens.purpose: Visual breakdown of finances through infographics.

states.empty: Illustration: 'Not enough data for insights yet.'

screens.layout: M3 data visualization layout. Large donut chart or bar chart taking up the top half. Below is a categorized list of spending. Use the brandPrimary, brandSecondary, and brandTertiary colors for data segments against the dark/light surface.

screens.name: Profile & Settings
screens.purpose: User preferences, dark mode toggle, and app info.
screens.layout: Cover banner at the top using a brand-tinted gradient. Profile avatar overlapping the bottom edge. Below are tonal grouped rows (surfaceSecondary) for settings, including the Dark Mode switch. List items use leading icons in brandTertiary tinted containers.

empty_state_illustration.alt: Empty wallet with a dollar note on a green background

profile_avatar.alt: Portrait of a professional man wearing a grey henley

instructions_to_main_agent: STRICT: No indigo, violet, purple, or any blue (including navy) allowed anywhere. Use the provided teal/emerald palette.
STRICT: Typography weight must never exceed 500. Do not use 'bold', '600', '700', etc. Hierarchy is established strictly through font size (using the scale provided) and color contrast.
STRICT: Implement a Dark Mode that uses the exact dark token pairs provided in the color object. Do not invert colors programmatically.
IMPLEMENTATION: Use tonal elevation (shadow_tier: 0) for cards. Backgrounds should be 'surfaceSecondary' instead of relying on heavy drop shadows. This is a core Material 3 behavior.
IMPLEMENTATION: Ensure the bottom tab bar and sticky headers use expo-blur (glassmorphism) when content scrolls behind them.

IMPLEMENTATION: In the Transactions list, use progressive disclosure. The list row shows Merchant Name, Amount, and Category. A single tap expands a @gorhom/bottom-sheet to show full details (Date, Time, Location) and actions (Edit/Delete).
IMPLEMENTATION: Make the Floating Action Button (FAB) prominent on the Dashboard and Transactions screens. It should be tinted with brandPrimary and open the Add Transaction flow.
IMPLEMENTATION: Whenever text is placed over the profile banner image or any background image, apply a vertical gradient scrim (transparent to ≥70% surfaceInverse) to guarantee WCAG AA readability.
IMPLEMENTATION: Wire up the haptic feedback mapping provided in the JSON directly to the Pressable components.

LEARNINGS FROM RESEARCH: Material 3 finance apps prioritize 'Functional Minimalism' and 'Data Storytelling'. Avoid cluttered tables. Use large typography for totals and narrative text ('You have spent 80% of dining') instead of raw pie charts alone. Use 'Dark Mode First' design logic where bright teal/emerald accents pop against deep grey/green surfaces.
